Strategy Description: A strategy that overweights companies with the greatest improvement in ESG ratings and underweights those with declining ratings.
Core Logic: The market reacts to changes in ESG ratings in a relatively short period. Firms improving their ESG profile are expected to outperform as the advantages of better ratings (e.g., increased cash flows) become apparent.
Entry Conditions: Calculate 12-month ESG momentum (change in ratings). Overweight (long) companies with the highest momentum relative to the MSCI World Index and underweight (short) those with the lowest/negative momentum.
Exit Conditions: Rebalanced monthly to capture short-term market reactions to rating changes.
Risk Management Rules: Uses the Barra Global Equity Model (GEM3) for portfolio construction with specific constraints.
Long/Short Logic: Long (overweight) high ESG momentum, short (underweight) low/negative ESG momentum.
Market Regime Suitability: Unknown; correlation to equity market risk is not explicitly detailed in source papers.
Performance Metrics (CAGR, Sharpe Ratio,: CAGR: 12.99% (indicative 2.23% active), Sharpe Ratio: 0.7 (indicative 0.89), Max Drawdown: 26%
Full Notes: Out-of-sample tests suggest the strategy's alpha may be deteriorating. The core idea is the change in rating rather than the absolute level.
